description: Genetic competence in B. subtilis is a transient developmental state allowing natural DNA uptake and transformation. The system is controlled by the master regulator ComK, which activates ~100 competence genes. ComK expression involves a complex regulatory circuit with positive autoregulation, negative regulation by MecA-ClpCP degradation, and bistable switching creating competent and non-competent subpopulations. ComS protein protects ComK from degradation under starvation conditions, initiating the competence program.

name: Competence Development and DNA Uptake
notes: 53 nodes showing ComK bistable switch with positive autoregulation. Contains 2 OR gates (starvation, density checks) and 1 AND gate (ComS AND ComK escape). Demonstrates heterogeneous competence in subpopulation (~10-20% cells become competent). Shows complete DNA uptake machinery.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified genetic and biochemical data from decades of research on B. subtilis competence. The ComK regulatory network and DNA uptake machinery are well-characterized. All regulatory proteins, feedback loops, and DNA uptake components have been validated against primary research sources.

sources.note: ComK bistable switch and competence heterogeneity
sources.title: Bistability in bacteria
Bistability in the Bacillus subtilis K-state (competence) system requires a positive feedback loop

sources.authors: Hamoen LW, Venema G, Kuipers OP
sources.title: Controlling competence in Bacillus subtilis: shared use of regulators
